skill.name: bullmq-specialist
skill.description: BullMQ expert for Redis-backed job queues, background processing, and reliable async execution in Node.js/TypeScript applications.
skill.summary: Expert guidance on BullMQ queue setup, job scheduling, worker patterns, and production-ready background processing architecture.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks as shell commands and text patterns as cryptographic issues. All findings are false positives - this is documentation-only content with no executable code. Safe for publication.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Markdown backticks used for inline code formatting, not shell execution

content.user_title: Build Scalable Job Queues with BullMQ
content.value_statement: Background processing bottlenecks slow down your application. This skill provides production-ready BullMQ patterns for reliable job queues, scheduled tasks, and high-throughput worker architectures.
actual_capabilities: Design and configure BullMQ queues with production settings
Implement delayed, scheduled, and repeatable job processing
Create job flows with parent-child dependencies
Configure worker concurrency and rate limiting
Handle job events, retries, and dead letter queues
Debug stuck jobs and optimize queue performance
limitations: Requires Redis server to be available and configured
Does not execute jobs - provides implementation guidance only
Focused on Node.js/BullMQ ecosystem, not other queue systems

use_cases.title: E-commerce Order Processing
use_cases.description: Set up queues to handle order confirmation emails, inventory updates, and payment processing asynchronously to improve checkout response times.

use_cases.title: Scheduled Report Generation
use_cases.description: Implement daily, weekly, or monthly report jobs that aggregate data and deliver insights without blocking user requests.

use_cases.title: AI Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Chain multiple AI processing steps together using job flows, handling rate limits and managing long-running inference tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Queue Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a BullMQ queue for processing user signup emails. I need connection settings, queue definition, and a basic worker that sends welcome emails.

prompt_templates.title: Delayed Job Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to schedule a follow-up email 3 days after user signup. Show me how to add a delayed job to the queue and configure the worker to process jobs at the correct time.

prompt_templates.title: Job Flow with Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a job flow where Step 1 validates data, Step 2 processes the validated data, and Step 3 sends a notification. Child jobs should only run if parent succeeds.

prompt_templates.title: Production Worker Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize my BullMQ worker for high throughput. Configure concurrency, rate limiting, retry strategies, and dead letter queue handling for a payment processing system.

output_examples.input: Set up a basic BullMQ queue for email processing
output: Queue configured with Redis connection pool
Worker processing jobs with concurrency of 10
Retry strategy: 3 attempts with exponential backoff
Event listeners for job completion and failures
Dead letter queue captures failed jobs after max retries
Manual retry endpoint available for operations team

best_practices: Store job payload references (IDs) instead of large data blobs to keep Redis memory efficient
Always configure dead letter queues to capture and analyze failed jobs
Set appropriate concurrency limits based on worker resources and downstream service capacity
anti_patterns: Giant job payloads that bloat Redis memory and slow down queue operations
No dead letter queue causing failed jobs to disappear without tracking
Infinite concurrency settings that overwhelm downstream services and databases

faq.question: What Redis version is required for BullMQ?
faq.answer: BullMQ requires Redis 6.2.0 or higher. Redis 7.x is recommended for production use with improved performance and stability.

faq.question: How do I handle job retries?
faq.answer: Configure the retry strategy when creating workers. BullMQ supports exponential backoff, fixed delays, or custom retry logic based on error types.

faq.question: Can jobs be prioritized?
faq.answer: Yes, BullMQ supports job priorities from 1-20. Higher priority jobs are processed before lower priority ones when the queue has pending jobs.

faq.question: How do I schedule recurring jobs?
faq.answer: Use BullMQ's repeatable jobs feature with cron expressions. This creates jobs automatically at specified intervals without manual scheduling.

faq.question: What happens if a worker crashes mid-job?
faq.answer: Jobs are automatically returned to the queue when a worker disconnects. The job can be retried by another worker based on your retry configuration.

faq.question: How do I monitor queue health?
faq.answer: Use BullMQ events for real-time monitoring, Redis commands for queue length checks, and consider Bull Board or custom dashboards for visualization.
